title: Slow-cooker lamb shoulder
subtitle: Bone-in shoulder cooked until the meat falls from the bone
excerpt: A whole bone-in lamb shoulder set on a bed of onion and garlic, slow-cooked with rosemary and stock for eight to ten hours until the meat falls away with no effort. The cooking juices reduce to an instant, intensely flavoured gravy. No carving required.
sourceNotes: Slow-roast lamb shoulder is a feature of British and Mediterranean Sunday cooking. The slow cooker version produces an equivalent result to a seven-hour oven braise with far less attention and lower energy use. The shoulder is the correct cut for this method, the leg is too lean and dries out at long cook times. Bone-in gives a richer result than boneless.

content.text: The shoulder will not fit upright in most slow cookers. Sit it on the vegetable bed at an angle or fold it to fit. It will shrink considerably during cooking. Eight hours is the minimum; ten produces a result where the meat slides off the bone entirely and can be pulled apart with a spoon.

Score the fat on the {{lamb-shoulder}} with a knife and season all over with {{sea-salt-fine}} and {{black-pepper}}, working it into the scores. Scatter the {{onion}} and {{garlic}} across the base of the slow cooker to form a trivet. Set the lamb on top. Add the {{rosemary}} and {{thyme-fresh}} on and around the lamb. Mix the {{worcestershire-sauce}} into the {{stock-chicken}} and pour around the base. Cook on low for 8 to 10 hours, until the meat falls from the bone when pressed. Transfer to a board and rest for 15 minutes. Strain the cooking juices into a small saucepan and reduce over high heat for 5 minutes for a loose gravy.

items.fix: Check the lid seals flat. For a leaner shoulder, add another 100 ml stock. Fat content varies between animals and seasons
items.cause: Lean shoulder, some variation between animals. Or the lid was not sealing

items.fix: Cook for a further hour or two, lamb shoulder cannot be overcooked. It will only become more tender
items.cause: The joint was colder than expected going in, or the slow cooker runs cool
items.symptom: Meat not falling from the bone at 8 hours

items.fix: Sear the shoulder in a very hot pan for 3 minutes per side before placing in the slow cooker. Not essential, but adds colour and depth to the gravy
items.cause: No browning or Maillard reaction in a wet cooker

content.text: Lamb shoulder in the slow cooker is a Sunday lunch that sets itself. Start it before midday and it is ready for dinner. The pulled texture means no carving at the table, spoon the meat onto plates with the braised onions and a spoonful of the reduced juices. Serve with roast potatoes and seasonal greens, or flatbread and yoghurt.
